perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Deliverable" means each item to be delivered by Wildform in accordance with Exhibit B hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Deliverable" means each item to be delivered by Wildform in accordance with Exhibit B hereto, excluding any software components.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Deliverable' includes all items listed in Exhibit B. By adding 'excluding any software components,' we create a contradiction because Exhibit B explicitly lists software items as deliverables. This will impact the obligations related to software deliverables within the contract, as Section 3.1 onwards refers to 'Deliverable' based on this modified definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Deliverables. Subject to payment by On2 of the Closing Date Payment Amount in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement, Wildform shall deliver each Deliverable in accordance with the timeframe (the "Timeframe") set forth in Exhibit B, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement. On2 will use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with Wildform as necessary in completing the Development Work and delivery of the Deliverables. Upon receipt of each Deliverable, On2 shall have a period of thirty (30) working days within which to inspect such Deliverable based upon the relevant Specification (an "Acceptance Period"). Should On2 find that any Deliverable does not substantially conform to the relevant Specification, On2 shall promptly so notify Wildform in writing, which notice shall provide sufficiently detailed explanation of the non-conformities so as to allow Wildform to reproduce them (any such notice is referred to herein as a "Nonconformance Notice"). Each of the Deliverables shall be deemed accepted by On2 unless On2 delivers to Wildform a Nonconformance Notice within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of such Deliverable. In the event that Wildform receives such Nonconformance Notice from On2, Wildform shall make commercially reasonable efforts to modify such Deliverable and re-deliver it to On2 after any such modification is completed. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to have been accepted by On2 when either (i) On2 notifies Wildform in writing of its acceptance of such Deliverables or (ii) On2 does not deliver a Nonconformance Notice to Wildform within the Acceptance Period. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, On2 shall not unreasonably withhold acceptance of any Deliverable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Deliverable" to explicitly exclude software components. However, section 3.1 and Exhibit B (incorporated by reference) treat software as a deliverable, creating a direct contradiction. This means that Wildform's obligation to deliver software as per section 3.1 is now unclear because the definition of 'Deliverable' in 1.4 has been narrowed.

perturbation.original_text: 1.14 "Specifications" shall mean the Flix Software customizations and integrations described on Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 "Specifications" shall mean the Flix Software basic features and design aspects, regardless of what is described on Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition ties the meaning of 'Specifications' directly to Exhibit B. By changing the definition to 'Flix Software basic features and design aspects, regardless of what is described on Exhibit B,' we create a contradiction. If Exhibit B lists specific customizations and integrations that go beyond the basic features, then the definition will cause confusion regarding what criteria On2 should use to inspect the deliverables. This contradicts Section 3.1 which references Specifications when determing Acceptance Period. By redefining 'Specifications,' it undermines the agreed-upon process, making it uncertain what On2 is entitled to receive and what Wildform is obliged to deliver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Deliverables. Subject to payment by On2 of the Closing Date Payment Amount in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement, Wildform shall deliver each Deliverable in accordance with the timeframe (the "Timeframe") set forth in Exhibit B, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement. On2 will use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with Wildform as necessary in completing the Development Work and delivery of the Deliverables. Upon receipt of each Deliverable, On2 shall have a period of thirty (30) working days within which to inspect such Deliverable based upon the relevant Specification (an "Acceptance Period"). Should On2 find that any Deliverable does not substantially conform to the relevant Specification, On2 shall promptly so notify Wildform in writing, which notice shall provide sufficiently detailed explanation of the non-conformities so as to allow Wildform to reproduce them (any such notice is referred to herein as a "Nonconformance Notice"). Each of the Deliverables shall be deemed accepted by On2 unless On2 delivers to Wildform a Nonconformance Notice within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of such Deliverable. In the event that Wildform receives such Nonconformance Notice from On2, Wildform shall make commercially reasonable efforts to modify such Deliverable and re-deliver it to On2 after any such modification is completed. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to have been accepted by On2 when either (i) On2 notifies Wildform in writing of its acceptance of such Deliverables or (ii) On2 does not deliver a Nonconformance Notice to Wildform within the Acceptance Period. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, On2 shall not unreasonably withhold acceptance of any Deliverable.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of "Specifications" tied it to Exhibit B, defining it as "Flix Software customizations and integrations described on Exhibit B." The changed definition, "Flix Software basic features and design aspects, regardless of what is described on Exhibit B," creates a direct conflict. Section 3.1 states that On2 will inspect Deliverables "based upon the relevant Specification." By decoupling "Specifications" from Exhibit B, the change makes it unclear what criteria On2 should use for inspection and what standards Wildform needs to meet to fulfill its obligations, directly contradicting the process outlined in Section 3.1. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 No Warranty. EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SUBSECTIONS 6.1 AND 6.2 ABOVE, THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE PROVIDED "AS-IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND AND BOTH PARTIES EXPRESSLY AGREE AND ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE BEING PROVIDED WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, ARISING OUT OF A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE OR OTHERWISE INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, FITNESS OR ADEQUACY FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE, QUALITY, PRODUCTIVENESS, CAPACITY, OR THAT THE OPERATION OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORTED TECHNOLOGY WILL BE ERROR-FREE. NO ORAL OR WRITTEN INFORMATION OR ADVICE GIVEN BY A PARTY, ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, LICENSORS, SUPPLIERS, AGENTS, OR TO ANYONE ELSE WHO HAS BEEN INVOLVED IN THE CREATION, PRODUCTION, LICENSING, SUBLICENSING, SUPPLY OR DELIVERY OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY WILL CREATE A REPRESENTATION, CONDITION, OR WARRANTY AND NEITHER PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON SUCH INFORMATION OR ADVICE.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 No Warranty. EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SUBSECTIONS 6.1 AND 6.2 ABOVE, THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE PROVIDED "AS-IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, except for a warranty of basic functionality for a period of 30 days following delivery, AND BOTH PARTIES EXPRESSLY AGREE AND ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE BEING PROVIDED WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, ARISING OUT OF A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE OR OTHERWISE INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, FITNESS OR ADEQUACY FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE, QUALITY, PRODUCTIVENESS, CAPACITY, OR THAT THE OPERATION OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORTED TECHNOLOGY WILL BE ERROR-FREE. NO ORAL OR WRITTEN INFORMATION OR ADVICE GIVEN BY A PARTY, ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, LICENSORS, SUPPLIERS, AGENTS, OR TO ANYONE ELSE WHO HAS BEEN INVOLVED IN THE CREATION, PRODUCTION, LICENSING, SUBLICENSING, SUPPLY OR DELIVERY OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY WILL CREATE A REPRESENTATION, CONDITION, OR WARRANTY AND NEITHER PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON SUCH INFORMATION OR ADVICE.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states "No Warranty." By adding "except for a warranty of basic functionality for a period of 30 days following delivery," we create an immediate contradiction. The initial part of the sentence negates all warranties, while the added clause introduces a limited warranty. This leads to confusion about what warranties, if any, apply to the deliverables and incorporated technology and creates uncertainty about On2's rights and remedies if the deliverables fail to function as expected within the first 30 days. This perturbation directly contradicts with the original spirit of "No Warranty"
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 No Warranty. EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SUBSECTIONS 6.1 AND 6.2 ABOVE, THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE PROVIDED "AS-IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, except for a warranty of basic functionality for a period of 30 days following delivery, AND BOTH PARTIES EXPRESSLY AGREE AND ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE BEING PROVIDED WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, ARISING OUT OF A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE OR OTHERWISE INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, FITNESS OR ADEQUACY FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE, QUALITY, PRODUCTIVENESS, CAPACITY, OR THAT THE OPERATION OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORTED TECHNOLOGY WILL BE ERROR-FREE. NO ORAL OR WRITTEN INFORMATION OR ADVICE GIVEN BY A PARTY, ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, LICENSORS, SUPPLIERS, AGENTS, OR TO ANYONE ELSE WHO HAS BEEN INVOLVED IN THE CREATION, PRODUCTION, LICENSING, SUBLICENSING, SUPPLY OR DELIVERY OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY WILL CREATE A REPRESENTATION, CONDITION, OR WARRANTY AND NEITHER PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON SUCH INFORMATION OR ADVICE.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.3 explicitly disclaims all warranties. The changed text introduces an exception, a "warranty of basic functionality for a period of 30 days following delivery." This directly contradicts the initial "No Warranty" statement, creating an in-text contradiction. Both the Changed Text and the Contradicted text are the same location, but the changes create an implicit contradiction to the 'No Warranty' clause in the same section itself.
